description: The alert indicates that the 'ps auxwww' command was executed by the 'www-data' user. This command is commonly used for process discovery to list all running processes on the system, which is a common behavior during the reconnaissance phase of an attack.
keywords: ps auxwww, process discovery, system information discovery
alert_desc: Processes running for all users were queried with ps command.

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:51:00 videoserver sshd[17243]: Accepted publickey for root from 10.12.0.222 port 40718 ssh2: ED25519 SHA256:erECc93zjoYONyq1nNcu4EwrYe78nzBExDzOmsokkrc

description: The alert indicates a successful SSH authentication for the root user from a remote IP address using public key authentication. This behavior relates to remote access and the use of valid accounts to access systems.
keywords: Valid Accounts, SSH, Remote Access

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:51:00 videoserver sshd[17243]: pam_unix(sshd:session): session opened for user root(uid=0) by (uid=0)

description: The alert indicates a successful SSH login session opened for the root user on the host videoserver. This behavior relates to authentication and session management, which can be analyzed for unauthorized access or lateral movement.
keywords: Valid Accounts, SSH, Remote System Services

original_alert.full_log: ossec: Agent stopped: 'videoserver->any'.

description: The Wazuh security agent on the host 'videoserver' has stopped, which may indicate an attempt to disable security monitoring or evade detection.
keywords: Impair Defenses, Indicator Removal on Host

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:53:21 videoserver sudo:     root : PWD=/usr/share/zoneminder/www ; USER=www-data ; COMMAND=/usr/bin/true

decoder.name: sudo
decoder.ftscomment: First time user executed the sudo command

description: The alert indicates that the root user executed a command as the www-data user via sudo for the first time on the system. This behavior involves privilege management and potential lateral movement or execution of commands under different user contexts.
keywords: sudo, privilege escalation, abuse of privilege

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:53:21 videoserver sudo:     root : PWD=/usr/share/zoneminder/www ; USER=www-data ; COMMAND=/usr/bin/zmdc.pl check

decoder.name: sudo
decoder.ftscomment: First time user executed the sudo command

description: The alert indicates a successful sudo execution where the root user executed the command /usr/bin/zmdc.pl check as the www-data user. This involves the use of sudo for privilege management and the execution of a specific ZoneMinder script.
keywords: sudo, privilege escalation, zmdc.pl

rule.description: New user added to the system.

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:56:00 videoserver useradd[1658]: new user: name=webadmin, UID=1003, GID=1003, home=/home/webadmin, shell=/bin/bash, from=none

description: The system detected the creation of a new user account named webadmin via the useradd command on the host videoserver. This activity is a common indicator of persistence or privilege escalation.
keywords: Create Account, Local Account
alert_desc: New user added to the system.

original_alert.full_log: Sep 22 18:59:00 videoserver sudo: webadmin : PWD=/home/webadmin ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/usr/bin/lspci

decoder.name: sudo
decoder.ftscomment: First time user executed the sudo command

description: The user webadmin successfully executed the command /usr/bin/lspci with root privileges via sudo on the host videoserver. This activity involves privilege escalation and system information discovery.
keywords: sudo, privilege escalation, lspci

description: The alert shows multiple HTTP GET requests containing directory traversal sequences (e.g., ../, %2e%2e/) targeting sensitive system files such as /etc/passwd, boot.ini, and winnt/repair/sam._. This indicates an attempt to access files outside the web root directory.
keywords: Path Traversal, Directory Traversal, Web Application Attack
alert_desc: Apache: Multiple Invalid URI requests from same source.

rule.description: Apache: Attempt to access forbidden directory index.

original_alert.full_log: [Mon Sep 22 18:30:44.603660 2025] [autoindex:error] [pid 661] [client 192.42.1.174:33500] AH01276: Cannot serve directory /var/www/default/log/: No matching DirectoryIndex (index.html,index.cgi,index.pl,index.php,index.xhtml,index.htm) found, and server-generated directory index forbidden by Options directive

description: The alert indicates an attempt to access a directory on an Apache web server where directory indexing is disabled. This behavior is often associated with reconnaissance or directory enumeration, where an attacker attempts to list files in sensitive or restricted directories to identify potential targets or configuration files.
keywords: Directory Listing, Reconnaissance, Discovery
alert_desc: Apache: Attempt to access forbidden directory index.

original_alert.full_log: Dec 15 11:10:45 inetfw kernel: [ 1638.994908] device ens3 entered promiscuous mode

description: The network interface ens3 on host inetfw has entered promiscuous mode, which allows the device to capture all network traffic passing through the interface. This behavior is often associated with network reconnaissance or traffic interception.
keywords: network sniffing, promiscuous mode, packet capture
